project.name: Bachelorprojekt (Workspace MVP)
project.description: A Kubernetes-based self-hosted collaboration platform for small teams. Integrates Nextcloud, Keycloak SSO, Collabora, Vaultwarden, LiveKit streaming, Brett 3D multiplayer game, Arena multiplayer server, and an Astro+Svelte website with native messaging, coaching pipeline, tracking system, and admin portal.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Specifies files and directories excluded from the Docker build context for the arena-server image, preventing unnecessary files from being sent to the Docker daemon.

nodes.name: CHANGELOG.md
nodes.summary: Auto-generated changelog tracking arena-server releases from v0.1.0 to v0.4.0 (2026-05-20), documenting features like powerups, items, zones, bots, and bug fixes for the multiplayer game server.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Three-stage Docker build for the arena-server Node.js application: a deps stage installs pnpm dependencies, a build stage compiles TypeScript to dist/, and a minimal runtime stage runs as non-root user on port 8090.
nodes.languageNotes: Multi-stage build with pnpm@9.15.3 via corepack; SQL migration files are explicitly copied from the build stage so they are available at runtime for the migrate runner.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Package manifest for the arena-server Node.js application, defining pnpm scripts for build, test, and start, and declaring dependencies including fastify, drizzle-orm, pg, jose, and vitest for testing.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Developer guide for the arena-server covering local development setup with pnpm and Postgres, running the vitest test suite, and deployment instructions via the project Taskfile.

nodes.name: jwks.ts
nodes.summary: Provides a cached JWKS (JSON Web Key Set) fetcher using jose's createRemoteJWKSet, keyed by issuer URL with a 5-minute TTL, used by the JWT verifier to resolve public keys from Keycloak.

nodes.name: getJwks
nodes.summary: Returns a cached JWKS key resolver for the given issuer, refreshing the cache if the entry is older than 5 minutes.

nodes.name: jwt.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the JWT verification layer, covering valid token acceptance, expired token rejection, wrong-issuer rejection, and wrong-audience rejection using RSA-signed JWTs.

nodes.name: jwt.ts
nodes.summary: Implements JWT verification for arena players using jose's jwtVerify, validating issuer, audience, and Keycloak realm_access roles, and extracting a stable player key from the JWT claims.

nodes.name: verifyArenaJwt
nodes.summary: Verifies a JWT token against the configured Keycloak issuer and audience, returning parsed player claims or null on any validation failure.

nodes.name: ai.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the BotAI decision logic, verifying that bots move toward enemies and loot when visible, and wander when the map is empty.

nodes.name: ai.ts
nodes.summary: Implements the BotAI class that drives NPC bot behavior in the arena game, including state-machine decision-making (hunt enemy, seek loot, flee zone, wander), A* pathfinding integration, and WASD input generation from computed angles.

nodes.name: BotAI
nodes.summary: Stateful bot AI controller that cycles through hunt/loot/flee/wander behaviors each decision tick, uses A* waypoints for navigation, and translates movement angles into discrete WASD input.

nodes.name: nav.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the navigation module, verifying A* pathfinding produces valid paths and the hasLos line-of-sight check correctly identifies blocked and clear sightlines.

nodes.name: nav.ts
nodes.summary: Provides A* pathfinding and line-of-sight utilities for the arena map, converting between world coordinates and grid cells, building walkability grids from wall data, and computing optimal waypoint paths.

nodes.name: astar
nodes.summary: Full A* pathfinding implementation on the navigation grid, returning a list of world-coordinate waypoints from a start position to a target, with nearest-walkable fallback for blocked targets.

nodes.name: config.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the loadConfig function, verifying that missing required environment variables throw errors and that defaults apply correctly.

nodes.name: config.ts
nodes.summary: Parses and validates required environment variables (PORT, DB_URL, OIDC_ISSUER, OIDC_AUDIENCE) for the arena-server, throwing descriptive errors for missing values.

nodes.name: loadConfig
nodes.summary: Reads and validates all required environment variables, returning a typed config object with port, dbUrl, oidcIssuer, and oidcAudience.

nodes.name: client.ts
nodes.summary: Creates and exports a Drizzle ORM database client factory wrapping a node-postgres Pool connection for the arena schema.

nodes.name: migrate.ts
nodes.summary: Implements a lightweight SQL migration runner that tracks applied migrations in a migrations_log table, reads SQL files from the migrations directory in sorted order, and applies them idempotently within transactions.

nodes.name: runMigrations
nodes.summary: Creates the migrations_log tracking table if absent, then iterates sorted SQL migration files and applies any not yet recorded, wrapped in per-migration transactions.

nodes.name: matches
nodes.summary: Core matches table storing arena game results including lobby code, timing, winner, map, player counts, and full results JSON blob.

nodes.name: match_players
nodes.summary: Per-player match result rows linking match_id to player_key with placement, kills, deaths, forfeit status, and character metadata.

nodes.name: lobbies
nodes.summary: Lobby state table tracking active and historical game lobbies with phase, host, expiry timestamps, and a JSONB state blob.

nodes.name: 0001_init.sql
nodes.summary: Initial database migration creating the matches, match_players, and lobbies tables for the arena game, along with three performance indexes on started_at, player_key, and lobby phase.

nodes.name: 0002_arena_app_grants.sql
nodes.summary: Grants SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE privileges on all arena schema tables to the arena application role, separating the migration user from the runtime service account.

nodes.name: repo.test.ts
nodes.summary: Integration tests for the arena repository layer, running against a real Postgres database to verify insertLobby, updateLobbyPhase, insertMatchWithPlayers, and getRecentMatches.

nodes.name: repo.ts
nodes.summary: Implements the arena data access layer with functions for lobby CRUD, transactional match+player insertion, and recent match retrieval, returned as a factory from makeRepo.

nodes.name: makeRepo
nodes.summary: Factory function returning an object of database access methods (insertLobby, updateLobbyPhase, insertMatchWithPlayers, getRecentMatches) bound to the provided pg Pool.

nodes.name: schema.ts
nodes.summary: Drizzle ORM schema definitions for the arena, matches, match_players, and lobbies tables, providing typed table objects for query building.

nodes.name: constants.ts
nodes.summary: Centralizes all game balance constants for the arena server including tick rate, map dimensions, player stats, weapon definitions, item/powerup spawn rates, zone parameters, and bot configuration.

nodes.name: items.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for item spawning, pickup detection, and item effect application (health, armor, ammo, key) in the arena game loop.

nodes.name: items.ts
nodes.summary: Handles the item system game loop ticks: spawning items on the map at configurable intervals, detecting player pickups by proximity, and applying item effects (health, armor, ammo, keys, weapon swaps).

nodes.name: map.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the static arena map layouts as wall rectangle arrays, including SOLID_WALLS (simple room) and CONCRETE_ARENA (multi-room tactical layout with item spawn spots and powerup positions).

nodes.name: physics.ts
nodes.summary: Provides all 2D physics primitives for the arena game: AABB collision detection, wall-sliding movement resolution, line casting for projectile/LOS checks, circle-AABB intersection, and distance/angle helpers.

nodes.name: moveWithCollision
nodes.summary: Moves a player hitbox by a velocity vector with wall-sliding collision resolution, testing X and Y axes independently to allow sliding along walls.

nodes.name: lineCast
nodes.summary: Casts a line segment through the wall list and returns the nearest hit fraction using slab-based AABB intersection, used for both projectile travel and line-of-sight checks.

nodes.name: powerups.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for powerup spawning, proximity pickup, active powerup expiry, and damage/speed multiplier effects in the arena game loop.

nodes.name: powerups.ts
nodes.summary: Manages the powerup system game loop: spawning powerups on a timer at map spots, detecting player pickups, applying effects (damage boost, speed boost, shield), tracking active durations, and expiring elapsed powerups.

nodes.name: state.ts
nodes.summary: Re-exports all shared game state type definitions (MatchState, PlayerState, WeaponState, ZoneState, etc.) from the shared proto/messages module, serving as the type entry point for server-side game logic.

nodes.name: tick.test.ts
nodes.summary: Integration tests for the full game tick loop, running a simulated match with human and bot slots to verify the tick engine completes without errors and produces consistent diff outputs.

nodes.name: tick.ts
nodes.summary: Core game loop engine for the Arena multiplayer server. The Tick class drives a fixed-interval simulation loop that processes player inputs, movement, weapon fire, melee, zone damage, item/powerup spawns, and elimination logic, then broadcasts state diffs to clients.

nodes.name: Tick
nodes.summary: Manages the server-side game tick for a single match: runs a setInterval loop, processes buffered player inputs, delegates to weapon/zone/powerup subsystems, eliminates players, and emits result events when the match ends.

nodes.name: buildDiff
nodes.summary: Computes a delta patch between two consecutive game states, returning an array of diff operations covering player positions, health, zone state, item and powerup spawns/despawns, and door transitions.

nodes.name: weapons.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the weapons subsystem, covering tryFireWeapon, tryMelee, applyDamage, and cooldown logic using Vitest.

nodes.name: weapons.ts
nodes.summary: Implements all server-side weapon mechanics: firing with line-of-sight raycasting, melee arc detection, ammo/cooldown ticking, reload, weapon pickup, and damage application with shield absorption.

nodes.name: tryFireWeapon
nodes.summary: Attempts to fire the shooter's active ranged weapon: checks ammo and cooldown, applies spread, performs wall linecast and per-player rayVsCircle intersection tests, returns hit results.

nodes.name: tryMelee
nodes.summary: Performs a melee sweep by computing distance and angle from attacker to each living player and returning players within the weapon's arc and range.

nodes.name: applyDamage
nodes.summary: Applies raw damage to a target player, respecting shield powerup absorption and clamping health and shield values to valid ranges.

nodes.name: zone.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the shrinking-zone subsystem, verifying zone initialization, tick-based shrink progression, and out-of-zone damage applied to players.

nodes.name: zone.ts
nodes.summary: Manages the battle-royale shrinking safe zone: initializes zone state, advances the shrink schedule each tick, and deals damage to players positioned outside the current zone boundary.

nodes.name: tickZone
nodes.summary: Advances the zone shrink state machine each game tick: emits zone-start events, interpolates the zone radius, applies per-tick damage to out-of-zone players, and emits zone damage events.

nodes.name: middleware.ts
nodes.summary: Express middleware factory for JWT authentication on HTTP routes, providing both requireUser (validates Bearer token via Keycloak JWT) and requireAdmin (checks arena-admin realm role) middleware functions.

nodes.name: routes.test.ts
nodes.summary: Integration tests for the HTTP route layer of the arena server, verifying lobby creation, listing, and match history endpoints.

nodes.name: routes.ts
nodes.summary: Defines all Express HTTP routes for the arena server: health check, lobby status, open/open-solo/open-1v3 lobby creation, single-match lookup, and recent matches list.

nodes.name: makeRoutes
nodes.summary: Factory function that wires all arena HTTP routes onto an Express Router, injecting lifecycle controller, registry, and repository dependencies.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Main entry point for the arena-server: bootstraps the Express app, runs database migrations, wires WebSocket server, broadcasters, and HTTP routes, then starts the HTTP listener and registers shutdown handlers.

nodes.name: botfill.ts
nodes.summary: Fills empty player slots in a lobby with AI bot entries up to the maximum player count, assigning bot-prefixed keys and auto-generated display names.

nodes.name: lifecycle.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the Lifecycle class covering lobby open, join, leave, start, and phase transitions using Vitest mocks.

nodes.name: lifecycle.ts
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full lifecycle of an arena lobby through phases (open → starting → in-match → slow-mo → results → closed), managing timers, bot filling, match tick creation, DB persistence, and rematch voting.

nodes.name: Lifecycle
nodes.summary: State machine controller for arena lobby phases: opens lobbies, handles join/leave/character selection, transitions through starting/in-match/slow-mo/results/closed phases with timers, and coordinates with the Tick engine and broadcaster.

nodes.name: registry.ts
nodes.summary: In-memory lobby registry providing CRUD helpers (getLobby, putLobby, removeLobby, activeLobby, listLobbies) and a random 4-digit code generator for lobby identification.

nodes.name: log.ts
nodes.summary: Exports a shared pino logger instance used across the entire arena-server codebase.

nodes.name: messages.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the arena WebSocket protocol message type definitions, verifying isClientMsg discriminator and protocol version constant.

nodes.name: messages.ts
nodes.summary: Defines all WebSocket protocol message types for the arena server (client and server messages), the PROTOCOL_VERSION constant, and the isClientMsg type guard used to validate incoming socket messages.
nodes.languageNotes: This file must remain byte-identical to website/src/components/arena/shared/lobbyTypes.ts — CI fails if they diverge (protocol drift guard).

nodes.name: broadcasters.ts
nodes.summary: Factory that creates Socket.IO broadcaster helpers for emitting lobby state, match snapshots, diffs, events, and match-end payloads to all clients in a lobby room.

nodes.name: handlers.ts
nodes.summary: Attaches all Socket.IO message handlers to a connected socket: join/solo-join/spectate room commands, start/character/rematch/forfeit/input actions routed to the Lifecycle controller, and a spectator subscription path.

nodes.name: attachHandlers
nodes.summary: Registers all Socket.IO event listeners for a single connected socket, routing join, game-action, input, and spectate messages to the appropriate Lifecycle or Tick methods.

nodes.name: server.ts
nodes.summary: Initializes the Socket.IO WebSocket server with rate limiting, CORS configuration, and JWT authentication middleware, then wires handler attachment on each connection.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration for the arena-server, targeting ES2022 with strict mode, module resolution bundler strategy, and source maps enabled.

nodes.name: vitest.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest configuration file for the arena-server test suite, setting up the test environment and globals.

nodes.summary: Docker build exclusion file for the brett service, preventing node_modules and development artifacts from being copied into the image.

nodes.name: package.json

nodes.name: combat-hud.mjs

nodes.name: startCombat
nodes.summary: Bootstraps the client-side combat system: mounts the HUD, binds keyboard and mouse event listeners for weapon selection and firing, subscribes to server damage/kill events via WebSocket, and handles respawn flow.

nodes.name: damage.mjs

nodes.name: fx.mjs
nodes.summary: Three.js visual effects module for the Brett combat system: spawns blood decals, muzzle flashes, slash arcs, smoke puffs, and fire sprites as short-lived 3D scene objects with automatic cleanup.

nodes.name: pickups.mjs
nodes.summary: Defines pickup item logic for the Mayhem combat mode, including randomized spawn position computation, pickup eligibility checks, and a PICKUP_TABLE constant defining collectible item types.

nodes.name: respawn.mjs
nodes.summary: Provides a Promise-based respawn overlay UI that displays a countdown timer in the DOM and resolves when the timer expires, used after player death in combat.

nodes.name: weapons.mjs

nodes.name: colors_and_type.css
nodes.summary: CSS stylesheet defining color palette variables and typographic rules for the figure-pack UI components used in the Brett 3D board application.

nodes.name: placement_spec.json
nodes.summary: Core placement specification for the figure pack, defining bone hierarchy, face variants, body shapes, accessory attachment points, coordinate system, and selection ring geometry used to assemble 3D mannequin figures.

nodes.name: catalog.html
nodes.summary: HTML catalog document for mentolder-brand game assets, listing available figure pack items and visual components used in the Brett 3D board application.

nodes.name: colors_and_type.css
nodes.summary: CSS stylesheet defining the mentolder-brand color palette and typography for game asset UI components, analogous to the figure-pack variant but scoped to the mentolder brand.

nodes.name: loadout-modal.mjs

nodes.name: showLoadoutModal

nodes.name: main.js
nodes.summary: Application entry point for the Brett game client that orchestrates FFA combat startup (loadout selection, dynamic imports of combat modules, touch joystick mounting) and solo Mayhem mode launch via URL navigation.

nodes.name: materials.js
nodes.summary: Defines and exports reusable Three.js MeshStandardMaterial presets (ink body, brass detail, warm wood, concrete, edge brass) and a helper to attach an author signature mesh to an object.

nodes.name: ai-bot.js
nodes.summary: Implements the MayhemAIBot class, an autonomous enemy bot that navigates the arena, targets nearby players, fires weapons, respawns on death, and integrates with the game mode and projectile systems.

nodes.name: MayhemAIBot
nodes.summary: Autonomous AI combat bot with per-frame tick logic for movement, targeting, firing, hit processing, and edge-based respawn.

nodes.name: audio.js
nodes.summary: Implements the MayhemAudio class that loads and plays positional Web Audio API sounds for gunfire, hits, blood splats, footsteps, and ambient fire loops with pitch randomization.

nodes.name: MayhemAudio
nodes.summary: Web Audio API manager for Mayhem combat sounds, handling async buffer loading, one-shot playback with gain/pitch variation, and ambient fire loop start/stop.

nodes.name: chase-camera.js
nodes.summary: Implements the ChaseCamera class, a third-person camera controller that handles pointer lock, mouse-look yaw/pitch accumulation, and smooth orbital position updates around the player.

nodes.name: ChaseCamera
nodes.summary: Third-person chase camera with pointer-lock mouse-look, pitch/yaw clamping, and per-frame orbital position calculation.

nodes.name: controls-panel.js
nodes.summary: Provides the in-game controls configuration panel UI with tabbed keybinding display, live rebinding support, a discovery banner, and an entry toast notification, all rendered as injected DOM overlays.

nodes.name: effects.js
nodes.summary: Implements the EffectsManager class and Particle helper for spawning Three.js visual effects including blood splats, blood decals, fireballs, impact dust clouds, and floating damage numbers.

nodes.name: EffectsManager
nodes.summary: Manages all particle and decal effects in the Mayhem arena, providing methods to spawn blood splats, fireballs, dust clouds, and canvas-rendered damage number popups.

nodes.name: game-mode.js
nodes.summary: Implements the GameModeManager class which handles all Mayhem game mode logic including FFA, Last-Man-Standing, and cooperative wave modes — tracking kills, deaths, respawn timers, enemy waves, and HUD data.

nodes.name: GameModeManager
nodes.summary: Central game mode controller supporting FFA, LMS, and cooperative wave modes with kill/death tracking, respawn timers, wave management, and HUD data aggregation.

nodes.name: keybindings.js
nodes.summary: Provides a MayhemKeybindings singleton on the window object with load, save, and getAction methods that persist customized key bindings to localStorage.

nodes.name: mayhem.js
nodes.summary: Core orchestrator for the Mayhem FFA/LMS/Coop combat mode — initializes all subsystems (camera, weapons, effects, AI bots, networking), drives the per-frame tick loop, handles WebSocket message dispatch, manages the HUD, and exposes the window.Mayhem global.
nodes.languageNotes: The largest single file in the Mayhem subsystem (790 lines); acts as a composition root wiring together 8+ subsystem classes via globals and closures rather than ES module imports.

nodes.name: obstacles.js
nodes.summary: Procedurally generates deterministic arena obstacles (pillars, crates, barrels, L-walls) from a seeded RNG keyed to the room token, and provides AABB geometry for collision detection.

nodes.name: physics.js
nodes.summary: Provides lightweight collision detection primitives (capsule-capsule, AABB-capsule overlap tests) and ragdoll physics integration helpers used by player avatars and projectiles.

nodes.name: player-avatar.js
nodes.summary: Implements the PlayerAvatar class managing health, hit detection, ragdoll physics, local movement, remote state interpolation, procedural animation, and weapon mesh attachment for a combat avatar.

nodes.name: projectiles.js
nodes.summary: Manages projectile lifecycle for the Mayhem mode — spawning fireball and bullet meshes, advancing them per frame, detecting obstacle and avatar collisions, triggering effects, and dispatching hit callbacks.

nodes.name: vehicle.js
nodes.summary: Implements the Vehicle class representing a drivable arena vehicle with AABB collision, impulse computation for ramming players, per-frame movement integration, and scene lifecycle management.

nodes.name: Vehicle
nodes.summary: Arena vehicle with linear movement, AABB collision bounds, and ramming impulse for player knockback.

nodes.name: weapons.js
nodes.summary: Implements the WeaponSystem class providing full weapon management for Mayhem — selection cycling, fire-rate gating, burst and automatic fire modes, scatter calculation, and weapon definition lookup.

nodes.name: WeaponSystem
nodes.summary: Weapon management controller handling selection, fire-rate enforcement, burst/auto fire modes with scatter, and weapon definition access.

nodes.name: mode-select.mjs
nodes.summary: Exports a Promise-based mode selection modal that lets the player choose a game mode (FFA, coop, etc.) before entering combat, writing the selection to modeState.

nodes.name: showModeSelect
nodes.summary: Renders a game-mode selection overlay, updates modeState on button click, and resolves the promise when the player confirms.

nodes.name: mode-state.mjs
nodes.summary: Exports createModeState factory that creates a shared event-emitting state object tracking the selected game mode and weapon loadout, with localStorage persistence.

nodes.name: createModeState

nodes.name: room-browser.js
nodes.summary: Implements the room browser UI overlay that fetches active game rooms from the server, renders them with player counts and mode labels, and handles room-join dialogs with mode selection and session storage routing.

nodes.name: scene.js
nodes.summary: The primary Three.js scene module for the Brett 3D board — builds the renderer, camera, lighting, floor, and mannequin figures; drives the main animation loop; handles WebSocket state sync, figure placement/IK, spring physics, walk animation, and Mayhem mode integration.
nodes.languageNotes: Near-1000-line monolithic scene file using Three.js directly (loaded via CDN importmap), with a hand-written CCD IK solver and spring-bone physics.

nodes.name: setLightIntensity
nodes.summary: Traverses the Three.js scene and adjusts light intensities based on the active mode (e.g., dim for Mayhem, normal for board mode).

nodes.name: talk-hpb.yaml
nodes.summary: Kubernetes manifests for the Nextcloud Talk High-Performance Backend (HPB): a Janus WebRTC gateway Deployment with a large ConfigMap for signaling server configuration (NATS, TURN, coturn integration) and a Service exposing ports 8080 and 8443.

nodes.name: talk-recording.yaml
nodes.summary: Kubernetes Deployment and Service for the Nextcloud Talk recording backend (spreed-recording), which joins calls to record audio/video and uploads the result to Nextcloud.

nodes.name: app.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI application that acts as a headless Whisper STT transcription bot for Nextcloud Talk: polls the Nextcloud DB for active calls, joins them via a headless Playwright browser, records audio chunks, sends them to Whisper, accumulates the transcript, and posts the final transcript to the Website API at call end.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses async Python with asyncio tasks for concurrent sessions; Playwright controls a headless Firefox in a virtual Xvfb display; PulseAudio provides the virtual audio sink for recording.

nodes.name: webhook
nodes.summary: Handles incoming Nextcloud Talk webhook events: verifies HMAC-SHA256 signature, parses the event payload, and starts or cancels transcription sessions based on call state changes.

nodes.name: run_session
nodes.summary: Orchestrates a full transcription session: allocates a virtual X display, spawns Xvfb and PulseAudio, starts a headless browser joining the Talk room, loops to record audio chunks and send them to Whisper, accumulates transcript segments, and triggers finalization on exit.

nodes.name: _db_get_meeting_resources
nodes.summary: Queries the Nextcloud database to retrieve all file resources (audio recordings, attachments) associated with a specific meeting token and time range, returning structured resource metadata.

nodes.name: _db_add_bot_to_missing_rooms
nodes.summary: Directly inserts the transcriber bot as a participant into Nextcloud Talk rooms that have active calls but are missing the bot, enabling auto-join without a Nextcloud API call for each room.

nodes.name: _finalize_and_teardown
nodes.summary: Finalizes a transcription session: assembles the full transcript text, queries the database for meeting resources and room metadata, posts the result to the Website API for DB/file storage, tears down the Xvfb/PulseAudio processes, and removes the session entry.

nodes.name: _whisper
nodes.summary: Sends an audio chunk file to the Whisper HTTP API and returns the transcription text with segment timestamps.

nodes.name: poll_loop
nodes.summary: Background asyncio loop that periodically queries active Nextcloud Talk call tokens and starts or stops transcription sessions via `tick` based on current call state.

nodes.name: auto_join_loop
nodes.summary: Background asyncio loop that runs every 30 seconds to ensure the transcriber bot is registered as a participant in all active Talk rooms, compensating for missed webhook events.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Single-stage Dockerfile for the talk-transcriber Python service: installs system deps (Xvfb, FFmpeg, PulseAudio, dbus), Python packages (FastAPI, Playwright, psycopg2, httpx), installs Firefox via Playwright, and starts uvicorn on port 8000.
nodes.languageNotes: Requires Xvfb, PulseAudio, and FFmpeg for headless browser audio recording — unusually heavy system-level dependencies for a Python web service.

nodes.name: teardown.sh
nodes.summary: Interactive Bash script that deletes the `workspace` Kubernetes namespace and the k3d cluster (defaulting to `workspace-dev`), with a confirmation prompt to prevent accidental destruction.

nodes.name: tests-retention-cronjob.yaml
nodes.summary: Kubernetes CronJob, ServiceAccount, Role, and RoleBinding that runs nightly to clean up old test-run artifacts (pods, secrets, configmaps) from the workspace namespace, preventing resource accumulation from test runs.

nodes.name: traefik-dashboard-dev.yaml
nodes.summary: Traefik-specific CRD resources (IngressRoute, Middleware, ServersTransport, TLSOption) exposing the Traefik dashboard at `traefik.localhost` in dev, protected by the oauth2-proxy forward-auth middleware.

nodes.name: Dockerfile (talk-transcriber config reference)
nodes.summary: Build configuration for the talk-transcriber container image.

nodes.name: vaultwarden-seed-credentials.yaml
nodes.summary: Kubernetes Secret holding the admin token and initial organisation credentials used by the Vaultwarden seed job to bootstrap the password manager with default values.

nodes.name: vaultwarden-seed-job.yaml
nodes.summary: Kubernetes Job manifest that runs a one-shot seed container to create initial collections, users, and credential entries in Vaultwarden via its API after first deployment.

nodes.name: vaultwarden.yaml
nodes.summary: Kubernetes manifests (PersistentVolumeClaim, Deployment, and Service) deploying Vaultwarden, a self-hosted Bitwarden-compatible password manager, with a 5Gi persistent volume and Keycloak SSO integration.
nodes.languageNotes: Multi-document YAML file combining PVC, Deployment, and Service resources; the parser falls back to regex extraction for multi-document files.

nodes.name: website-dev-secrets.yaml
nodes.summary: Kubernetes Secret containing development-only plaintext credentials for the website service including database password, OIDC client secret, SMTP credentials, and JWT signing key.

nodes.name: website-rbac.yaml
nodes.summary: Kubernetes RBAC manifests defining ServiceAccount, Role, ClusterRole, RoleBinding, and ClusterRoleBinding for the website pod, granting it read access to Kubernetes resources like nodes and pods for the admin monitoring dashboard.

nodes.name: website-schema.yaml
nodes.summary: Large Kubernetes ConfigMap containing the full database schema SQL for the website's PostgreSQL database, including table definitions, indexes, functions, and triggers for the coaching platform's data model.
nodes.languageNotes: 1663-line ConfigMap embedding raw SQL schema; the single-document structure means the YAML parser reads the entire schema as a single data block.

nodes.name: website-seller-config.yaml
nodes.summary: Kubernetes ConfigMap providing seller-specific configuration values (contact information, pricing tiers, and feature flags) used by the website to customise the coaching service offering per deployment.

nodes.name: website.yaml
nodes.summary: Primary Kubernetes manifest bundle for the Astro website service: includes a large environment ConfigMap, Namespace, ServiceAccount, Role/ClusterRole/RoleBinding, Deployment, Service, and multiple Traefik IngressRoute and Middleware resources for routing, authentication, and rate-limiting across dev and prod.
nodes.languageNotes: 688-line multi-document YAML combining ~15 Kubernetes resources; the namespace is 'website' (separate from the main 'workspace' namespace).

nodes.name: whiteboard.yaml
nodes.summary: Kubernetes manifests deploying the Nextcloud Whiteboard standalone server (a Node.js WebSocket service) with a Deployment and ClusterIP Service, supporting collaborative drawing in the workspace.

nodes.name: .build-trigger
nodes.summary: Sentinel file touched by CI to force a Docker image rebuild when the website source has not otherwise changed, typically containing a timestamp or commit hash.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Docker build context exclusion file listing paths (node_modules, dist, .env files, test output) to omit from the image build context, keeping the build fast and secure.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Template file documenting all required environment variables for the Astro website, covering Keycloak OIDC, SMTP, external service URLs, and the site base URL.

nodes.name: .rebuild
nodes.summary: Marker file used to trigger a full website rebuild on next deploy, analogous to .build-trigger but may carry additional rebuild instructions or flags.

nodes.name: astro.config.mjs
nodes.summary: Astro framework configuration enabling the Svelte integration and setting the output mode to 'server' for SSR with the Node.js adapter, establishing the core build pipeline for the website.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses ESM module syntax (.mjs); configures Astro SSR with @astrojs/svelte and @astrojs/node adapters for server-side rendering.

nodes.name: CHANGELOG.md
nodes.summary: Auto-generated version history for the website package spanning versions 1.1.0 through 1.4.1 (2026-05-20), tracking features and bug fixes with conventional-commits-style entries.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Two-stage Docker build: a 'build' stage compiles the Astro SSR website with Node 22-alpine, and a minimal 'runtime' stage copies the dist, node_modules, test scripts, and knowledge scripts, exposing port 4321 and running as the non-root node user.
nodes.languageNotes: Multi-stage build reduces final image size; the runtime stage installs bash/curl/jq via apk for in-container test execution and knowledge script support.

nodes.name: 2026-04-10-client-portal.md
nodes.summary: Detailed 6-task implementation plan for the client portal feature, covering Playwright test scaffolding, auth helpers, Nextcloud file integration, portal tab components, the /portal page, and /admin dashboard with client detail view.

nodes.name: 2026-04-10-document-signing.md
nodes.summary: 5-task implementation plan for DocuSeal-backed document signing, covering E2E tests, the POST /api/signing/confirm endpoint, a document viewer page, the SignaturesTab component, and Nextcloud folder bootstrapping.

nodes.name: 2026-04-10-meeting-history.md
nodes.summary: 6-task implementation plan for the meeting history feature, covering Playwright tests, meetings-db.ts extensions, audio cleanup after transcription, the POST /api/meeting/release endpoint, and client and admin tab components.

nodes.name: 2026-04-10-slot-widget.md
nodes.summary: 4-task implementation plan for an appointment slot widget on the homepage, covering the Playwright test, SlotWidget.astro component, homepage integration, and URL-parameter pre-fill in the booking form.

nodes.name: 2026-04-10-client-portal-design.md
nodes.summary: Design specification for the client portal covering overview, architecture, /portal and /admin page layouts, authentication and error handling, data flow between Keycloak/Nextcloud/DocuSeal, and the list of new files to create.

nodes.name: 2026-04-10-document-signing-design.md
nodes.summary: Design specification for document signing via DocuSeal, detailing admin and client flows, the confirmation record format, data flow, security model (JWT-signed tokens), and new files required.

nodes.name: 2026-04-10-meeting-history-design.md
nodes.summary: Design specification for the meeting history release model, covering admin and client flows, data model (with hold_until timestamp), data flow from Nextcloud Talk transcription to portal display, and new and modified files.

nodes.name: 2026-04-10-slot-widget-design.md
nodes.summary: Design specification for the homepage slot widget, covering behaviour, UI placement above existing hero content, booking form integration via URL parameters, error/empty states, and brand-aware rendering.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: npm package manifest for the Astro website defining build, dev, preview, and test scripts, along with production dependencies (Astro, Svelte, pg, sharp, nodemailer, pdfkit, ws) and dev dependencies (TypeScript, Playwright).

nodes.name: pnpm-workspace.yaml
nodes.summary: Minimal pnpm workspace configuration allowing native builds (e.g., sharp, canvas) within the website package.

nodes.name: app.css
nodes.summary: Korczewski brand application-level CSS providing utility classes, layout helpers, and component overrides that complement the main kore design system for in-app pages (portal, admin).

nodes.name: colors_and_type.css
nodes.summary: Korczewski brand color palette and typography CSS custom properties, defining the full design token set including primary/secondary/accent colors, semantic text colors, and font stacks for the kore design system.
nodes.languageNotes: Serves as the design token foundation consumed by kore-app.css and kore-website.css; 253 lines of CSS custom property definitions.

nodes.name: kore-app.css
nodes.summary: Core Kore design system stylesheet for application views (portal, admin dashboard), providing component styles for navigation, cards, tables, modals, buttons, form inputs, and the sidebar layout used on korczewski.de.
nodes.languageNotes: 562 lines; the Kore design system is specific to the korczewski brand and is distinct from the mentolder Hero/WhyMe/ServiceRow component styles.

nodes.name: kore-website.css
nodes.summary: Kore design system stylesheet for public-facing website pages on korczewski.de, providing hero sections, PR timeline, feature cards, footer, and responsive navigation styles distinct from in-app views.
nodes.languageNotes: Paired with kore-app.css; this file handles marketing/public pages while kore-app.css handles authenticated application pages.

nodes.name: build
nodes.summary: Docker build stage using node:22-alpine that installs npm dependencies and compiles the Astro website to the dist/ directory.

nodes.name: runtime
nodes.summary: Docker runtime stage using node:22-alpine that copies compiled artifacts from the build stage and serves the Astro SSR app on port 4321 as the non-root node user.

nodes.name: site.webmanifest
nodes.summary: Progressive Web App manifest for the korczewski brand, defining app name, icons, theme color, and display settings for installable PWA support.

nodes.name: questionnaire.html
nodes.summary: Standalone HTML questionnaire template for korczewski brand starters, rendered as a self-contained page with embedded styles and form logic for client intake assessments.

nodes.name: colors_and_type.css
nodes.summary: Core brand CSS for mentolder defining all color tokens (CSS custom properties), typography scale, and font declarations that establish the mentolder visual identity across the platform.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses CSS custom properties (variables) extensively for theming, enabling consistent brand colors and typography across all mentolder pages.

nodes.name: print.css
nodes.summary: Print stylesheet for mentolder brand pages, overriding screen styles with print-optimized layout, hiding navigation/interactive elements, and setting appropriate page margins.

nodes.name: questionnaire.html
nodes.summary: Standalone HTML questionnaire template for mentolder brand starters, providing a self-contained client intake form with embedded mentolder branding and styles.

nodes.name: gerald.webp
nodes.summary: Static WebP image asset served as a public resource, likely a profile or team photo used in the website's public-facing pages.

nodes.name: robots.txt
nodes.summary: Robots exclusion file instructing web crawlers on which paths may or may not be indexed, controlling SEO visibility for the mentolder/korczewski website.

nodes.name: systembrett.whiteboard
nodes.summary: Nextcloud Whiteboard export file containing a pre-built systemic constellation board layout used as a starter template for the Brett 3D board feature.

nodes.name: gen-einvoice-fixtures.ts
nodes.summary: Development script that generates Factur-X/ZUGFeRD e-invoice XML test fixtures for four tax scenarios (Kleinunternehmer, Regelbesteuerung, mixed-rate, reverse-charge EU) and writes them to the test/fixtures directory.

nodes.name: gobd-backfill.mjs
nodes.summary: One-shot idempotent database backfill script that populates missing GoBD-required fields (belegnummer and skr_konto) on legacy eur_bookings rows using SESSIONS_DATABASE_URL.

nodes.name: seed-questionnaires.mjs
nodes.summary: Database seeding script that populates the questionnaire tables with three standard coaching assessments: Thomas-Kilmann conflict model, Riemann-Thomann personality model, and the Inneres Funktionsmodell, connecting to the database via DNS-resolved hostname.

nodes.name: seedIfAbsent
nodes.summary: Guard function that checks if a questionnaire with the given title already exists before running the seed function, preventing duplicate inserts on re-runs.

nodes.name: seedThomasKilmann
nodes.summary: Seeds the Thomas-Kilmann conflict mode instrument questionnaire including dimensions (competing, collaborating, compromising, avoiding, accommodating) and their associated answer options.

nodes.name: seedRiemannThomann
nodes.summary: Seeds the Riemann-Thomann personality model questionnaire with its four axis dimensions and bipolar answer pairs for use in coaching assessments.

nodes.name: seedInneresFunktionsmodell
nodes.summary: Seeds the Inneres Funktionsmodell (inner function model) questionnaire, a German coaching tool for assessing internal psychological functions and their dimensions.

nodes.name: systemtest-job.ts
nodes.summary: CLI entrypoint for system-test maintenance tasks with two modes: cleanup-fixtures (purges test data and expired magic tokens) and drain-outbox (drains the failure-bridge outbox and runs the reconciler), usable for local debugging or manual pod-exec runs.

nodes.name: sRGB.icc
nodes.summary: Standard sRGB ICC color profile embedded in the website assets, used for accurate PDF/print color management in e-invoice or document generation workflows.

nodes.name: AdminBookingModal.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin Svelte 5 modal component for booking services on behalf of clients, featuring client search/selection, time slot browsing by week, project assignment, and form submission with pre-fill support for known clients.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Svelte 5 runes ($props, $state, $derived) throughout for reactive state management.

nodes.name: AdminMeetingModal.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin Svelte 5 modal for scheduling new meetings with clients, supporting client search, manual attendee entry for external participants, project linking, and meeting time/format selection.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Svelte 5 runes; addresses tickets T000161 and T000164.

nodes.name: AdminShortcuts.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin Svelte 5 component for managing a user-defined list of quick-access shortcut links, with inline create, edit, reorder, and delete functionality including timed error display.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Svelte 5 runes; implements optimistic error handling with auto-dismiss timer.

nodes.name: AssetGallery.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin Svelte component providing a filterable gallery of uploaded assets (images, audio, video, documents) with copy-to-clipboard support for asset paths.

nodes.name: BillingCustomerLeitwegForm.svelte
nodes.summary: Small admin Svelte form component for capturing the German Leitweg-ID (XRechnung routing identifier) required for B2G e-invoicing, embedded within billing customer forms.
nodes.languageNotes: Leitweg-ID is a German public procurement routing identifier used in the XRechnung standard.

nodes.name: BookReader.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin Svelte component for reading ingested coaching books chunk-by-chunk with infinite scroll, text selection to create knowledge items, and cluster assignment for extracted passages.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements text-selection-based knowledge extraction by listening to document mouseup events and computing selection rectangles for a floating action menu.

nodes.name: BookUploadForm.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin Svelte form component for uploading coaching books (PDF/EPUB) to the coaching pipeline, capturing title, author, and slug metadata before triggering the ingest workflow.

nodes.name: ClientContractsPanel.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin Svelte panel component displaying a client's contract history with status badges, download links for signed documents, and filtering by contract state.

nodes.name: ClientQuestionnairesPanel.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin Svelte panel showing a client's completed questionnaire responses with expandable detail views, enabling coaches to review assessment results per client.

nodes.name: CoachingSettings.svelte
nodes.summary: Complex admin Svelte 5 settings panel for the coaching AI pipeline, managing KI provider configurations (API keys, model params, DSGVO EU-endpoint) and session step prompt templates across two tabs.
nodes.languageNotes: Supports multi-provider LLM configuration including Claude (thinking mode), Mistral (safe prompt), and custom providers with configurable field visibility per provider type.

nodes.name: ProjectDetail.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin Svelte component showing the detail view of a single coaching project, displaying metadata, linked client, status, and associated sessions with navigation to the session wizard.

nodes.name: ProjectsOverview.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin Svelte component listing all coaching projects in a filterable table with status badges, client names, and links to individual project detail views.

nodes.name: SessionsOverview.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin Svelte component listing coaching sessions with phase progress indicators, status chips, AI provider labels, and direct links into the SessionWizard for continuing or reviewing sessions.

nodes.name: SessionWizard.svelte
nodes.summary: Complex admin Svelte 5 multi-step coaching session wizard that walks coaches through each AI-assisted session step, streaming responses from the configured LLM provider and persisting coach inputs and notes per step.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements SSE-based streaming for Claude provider; uses phase color coding (problem_ziel, analyse, loesung, umsetzung) to visually track progress through the session framework.

nodes.name: CollectionMergePanel.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin Svelte component triggered via custom DOM event that allows merging multiple knowledge collections into a new named collection, with brand assignment and conflict prevention for cross-model merges.
nodes.languageNotes: Opens via a window custom event ('open-collection-merge') rather than direct prop, enabling decoupled trigger from any page.

nodes.name: Components.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin Svelte component providing a filterable CRUD registry of platform components (physical and non-physical), tracking their deployment cluster (mentolder/korczewski/both), status, and URLs.

nodes.name: CreateInvoiceModal.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin Svelte 5 modal for creating invoices or quotes, supporting internal client lookup, external recipient entry with Leitweg-ID support, service/quantity selection, and both invoice and quote modes.
nodes.languageNotes: Exports ServiceOption interface for typed prop passing from parent pages; supports XRechnung (B2G) fields when Leitweg-ID is provided.

nodes.name: DatevExportWidget.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin widget for exporting DATEV-format accounting data (Buchungsstapel) as CSV download or emailing it directly to a tax advisor, with year/month selection controls.

nodes.name: DokumentEditor.svelte
nodes.summary: Multi-section admin editor combining newsletter management, contract template CRUD (with HTML editor and DIN-A4 iframe preview), and questionnaire template editing via tabbed navigation.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Svelte 5 $state runes alongside Svelte 4 $effect, and renders a live DIN-A4 iframe preview of HTML contract templates.

nodes.name: DraftsInbox.svelte
nodes.summary: Three-panel inbox UI for reviewing AI-classified coaching knowledge drafts (reflections, dialog patterns, exercises, case examples) — allows accept, publish, or reject actions with inline payload editing and acceptance-rate badge.
nodes.languageNotes: Three-column grid layout collapses to stacked single-column on mobile with responsive CSS. Uses Svelte 4 reactive syntax ($:) with onMount.

nodes.name: EurReport.svelte
nodes.summary: Compact Einnahmen-Überschuss-Rechnung (EÜR) card displaying income, expenses, profit/loss, VAT collected, and input tax for a given year, loaded from the bookkeeping summary API.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Svelte 5 $props and $state runes.

nodes.name: AngeboteSection.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin CMS section for managing service offering cards (Leistungskarten) with full CRUD, ordering, per-service page content editing (headline, intro, sections, pricing, FAQ), and a service category catalog editor.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Svelte 5 $state and $props runes throughout. Dense inline helper functions for slug generation, reordering, and nested pageContent mutations.

nodes.name: CoachingSection.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin CMS editor for the Coaching page content (headline, intro, focus topics, for-whom list, CTA), saving to the coaching content API endpoint.

nodes.name: CustomSection.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin editor for user-defined custom website sections with dynamic field rendering (text, textarea, URL fields), save/delete actions, and an onDeleted callback to the parent.

nodes.name: FaqSection.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin CMS editor for the FAQ page section, supporting add/remove/reorder of question-answer pairs with inline save to the FAQ API.

nodes.name: FuehrungSection.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin CMS editor for the Führung & Persönlichkeit service page content, with fields for headline, intro, focus areas, for-whom list, and CTA text/link.

nodes.name: KontaktSection.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin CMS editor for the contact page section (name, title, email, phone, address, availability), saving contact content to the contacts API.

nodes.name: RechnungsvorlagenSection.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin editor for invoice template configuration — intro/outro text, Kleinunternehmer tax notice, and email subject/body templates used for invoicing.

nodes.name: RechtlichesSection.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin CMS editor for legal pages (Impressum, Datenschutz, AGB, Widerruf), supporting both auto-generated and fully custom HTML content per legal document type.

nodes.name: ReferenzenSection.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin CMS editor for managing client references/testimonials (name, role, company, quote, rating), with add/remove/reorder and save functionality.

nodes.name: ServicePageSection.svelte
nodes.summary: Universal admin editor for individual service page content (50+ digital, KI-Transition, Beratung), covering card metadata, headline, intro, for-whom list, content sections, pricing tiers, FAQ items, and CTA.
nodes.languageNotes: Reused across multiple service page types via slug/pageLabel props. Uses Svelte 5 $state and $props runes.

nodes.name: StartseiteSection.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin CMS editor for the homepage hero section content (headline, subheadline, intro text, CTA text/link, hero image URL), saving to the homepage content API.

nodes.name: UebermichSection.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin CMS editor for the 'About me' page section (bio text, credentials list, profile image URL, quote), saving to the about-me content API.

nodes.name: VertragsvorlagenSection.svelte
nodes.summary: Dedicated admin section for contract template management (Vertragsvorlagen) with CRUD operations, HTML editor, DIN-A4 iframe preview, stand-date picker, DocuSeal integration display, and starter template loading.

nodes.name: InhalteEditor.svelte
nodes.summary: Top-level admin content editor that orchestrates all website CMS sections (startseite, coaching, FAQ, kontakt, rechtliches, etc.) plus newsletter, questionnaires, contracts, and invoices via a two-level tab/section navigation system with URL state sync.
nodes.languageNotes: Acts as an orchestrator/router composing 15+ child section components. Uses Svelte 5 $state, $effect, and $props runes. URL search params are synced to preserve active tab and section on reload.

nodes.name: KnowledgeJsonImport.svelte
nodes.summary: Modal component for importing a JSON knowledge collection via streaming SSE upload, showing chunk-by-chunk progress and listening for a global 'open-json-import-modal' window event as its trigger.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the Fetch API with a ReadableStream reader for chunked SSE progress events. Event-driven open via window.addEventListener.

nodes.name: KnowledgeSourceModal.svelte
nodes.summary: Modal for creating a new knowledge source collection, supporting text paste or PDF upload (up to 25 MB), brand scoping (mentolder/korczewski/both), and notifying the parent via onCreated callback.

nodes.name: BerichteTab.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin monitoring tab displaying test run history with pass/fail/skip counts and downloadable results, plus a panel for manually uploaded test protocols via TestResultsPanel.

nodes.name: BugsTab.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin bug ticket management tab with filterable list (status, category, search), expandable detail rows showing description, screenshots, threaded comments, and workflow actions (resolve, archive, reopen).
nodes.languageNotes: Lazy-loads per-ticket comments on expand. Comment state is maintained in a Record keyed by ticket ID to avoid re-fetch.

nodes.name: ClusterTab.svelte
nodes.summary: Kubernetes cluster monitoring tab showing node resource usage with colored progress bars, pod status summary (running/pending/restarting/failed), a pod table, and recent warning events with inline bug-ticket creation.
nodes.languageNotes: Auto-refreshes every 15 seconds via setInterval with cleanup on onDestroy. Integrates bug ticket creation directly from Kubernetes warning events.

nodes.name: DeploymentsTab.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin monitoring tab for Kubernetes deployment status, supporting restart and scale actions via confirmation modals, with a 15-second auto-refresh cycle.

nodes.name: FlakeMatrix.svelte

nodes.languageNotes: Uses Svelte 5 $state runes. Pure visual component driven by /api/admin/tests/flake.

nodes.name: LogsTab.svelte

nodes.name: OverviewTab.svelte
nodes.summary: Dashboard overview tab showing pod/node/deployment health summary cards and recent warning events, with navigation dispatch events to jump to the cluster, deployments, or logs tabs.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses createEventDispatcher to emit 'navigate' events consumed by the parent monitoring page. Auto-refreshes every 15 seconds.

nodes.name: PlaywrightPanel.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin panel that displays the latest Playwright E2E test report in an iframe if available, or shows a copyable Claude prompt for running E2E tests and posting the HTML report via webhook.

nodes.name: TestDataPanel.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin panel for generating and purging tagged test data (customers, invoices, meetings, bookings) with confirmation modal before destructive purge, displaying a status summary message.

nodes.name: TestRunner.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin test runner for executing BATS bash tests (FA-*, SA-*, NFA-*) against prod or local tier via SSE streaming — shows live log output, per-test results table, summary bar, and provides JSON/MD download of results.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses browser EventSource API for SSE streaming of test log lines and results. Supports test ID filtering for targeted runs.

nodes.name: TestsTab.svelte
nodes.summary: Monitoring tab shell that composes TrendChart, FlakeMatrix, TraceabilityMatrix, TestRunner, and PlaywrightPanel into a single stacked layout for the test monitoring section.

nodes.name: TraceabilityMatrix.svelte
nodes.summary: Displays a filterable traceability matrix mapping test IDs to source files and their last pass/fail/skip status, fetched from /api/admin/tests/traceability.

nodes.name: TrendChart.svelte
nodes.summary: Renders a 30-day pass/fail/skip trend as an inline SVG polyline chart using data from /api/admin/tests/trend.

nodes.name: NewsletterAdmin.svelte
nodes.summary: Full newsletter management panel with three tabs: subscriber list management (add/delete/filter), campaign list with template reuse, and an HTML compose editor with live server-rendered preview and send confirmation.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Svelte 5 $state/$effect runes for reactivity; preview is fetched server-side via /api/admin/newsletter/preview for accurate branded rendering matching actual sends.

nodes.name: DatenbankTab.svelte
nodes.summary: Ops tab for database backup and restore: lists Kubernetes backup CronJob runs per cluster, triggers immediate backups, and provides a modal for selecting a backup and target database to restore.
nodes.languageNotes: Restore action requires explicit checkbox confirmation to guard against accidental data loss; supports both mentolder and korczewski clusters.

nodes.name: DienstTab.svelte
nodes.summary: Ops tab displaying all Kubernetes Deployments grouped by namespace label, with per-deployment restart and scale actions guarded by a confirmation dialog; auto-polls every 30 seconds.

nodes.name: DnsZertTab.svelte

nodes.name: AssetModal.svelte

nodes.name: HardwareTab.svelte
nodes.summary: Platform tab rendering a table of hardware asset records (physical nodes) with their live Kubernetes node status, role, location, IP, and OS information.

nodes.name: HealthTab.svelte
nodes.summary: Platform health overview tab that displays per-cluster service health probes with latency indicators fetched from /api/admin/ops/health.

nodes.name: SoftwareTab.svelte

nodes.name: PlatformHub.svelte

nodes.name: ProjectMeetingsTab.astro
nodes.summary: Astro component displaying a collapsible list of project meetings with transcripts, AI insights, and artifacts; includes a native dialog for assigning unassigned meetings to the current project via PATCH calls.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a hybrid Astro SSR template with an inline <script> block for client-side interactivity (modal open/close and PATCH-on-click), since full hydration is not needed.

nodes.name: ProjectQuestionnairesPanel.astro
nodes.summary: Shared Astro panel used on project and ticket detail pages to display active and archived questionnaire assignment bundles, delegating each card to QuestionnaireBundleCard.

nodes.name: PublishEditor.svelte

nodes.languageNotes: Validates quote length via validateQuoteLength to ensure legal compliance with §51 UrhG before enabling the publish button.

nodes.name: QuestionnaireBundleCard.astro
nodes.summary: Astro card component rendering a single questionnaire assignment with dimension score bar charts, coach notes, and a collapsible Q&A section; links to the full review page.

nodes.summary: Full CRUD editor for questionnaire templates: lists existing templates, and provides an inline editor for metadata, scoring dimensions, and questions with answer-option/dimension mapping and publish/draft workflow.
nodes.languageNotes: Supports a test_step question type that captures expected results and function URLs alongside the standard ab_choice/ja_nein/likert_5 types.

nodes.name: RecordPaymentModal.svelte
nodes.summary: Modal dialog for recording a payment against an invoice, supporting bank transfer, SEPA, cash, and other methods with a mandatory note requirement for negative (correction) bookings.

nodes.name: SeoEditor.svelte
nodes.summary: SEO meta-tag editor allowing per-page title and description management for all mentolder.de pages, with live character-count indicators and 120–160 character guidance for descriptions.

nodes.summary: Compact widget displaying the current year's revenue progress against the §19 UStG Kleinunternehmer threshold with a color-coded progress bar and alert messages at warning and exceeded levels.

nodes.name: TestResultsPanel.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin panel displaying manual system test results grouped by questionnaire template, with collapsible step details, last-run timestamps, and a modal for creating bug tickets directly from failed steps.

nodes.name: TicketActionBar.svelte
nodes.summary: Action bar on the ticket detail page providing three expandable panels: status transition with optional resolution and note, comment posting with public/internal visibility toggle, and ticket linking with relationship kind and optional PR number.

nodes.name: TicketActivityTimeline.svelte
nodes.summary: Read-only chronological timeline component rendering ticket events: creation, field updates with diff view, internal/public comments, ticket links, and merged PRs.

nodes.name: TicketQuickEdit.svelte

nodes.name: TicketsTab.svelte

nodes.name: TicketsTableBody.svelte
nodes.summary: Table body component for the full ticket list view, rendering rows with inline status select, attention-mode pill (AI-ready/auto/needs-human), and an expandable AI-question/answer widget for tickets awaiting human input.
nodes.languageNotes: Maintains per-row state in a Map keyed by ticket ID to support concurrent optimistic updates; answer submission auto-promotes the ticket to ai_ready attention mode.

nodes.name: WebCrawlSourceModal.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin modal component for configuring and submitting web crawl source URLs to the knowledge ingestion pipeline.

nodes.name: WissenHub.svelte
nodes.summary: Admin knowledge hub dashboard component for managing coaching knowledge sources, displaying ingestion status, and triggering classify/ingest workflows.

nodes.name: AdminEinstellungenTabs.astro
nodes.summary: Astro component rendering tabbed navigation for the admin settings page, linking to Einstellungen sub-sections.

nodes.name: ArenaBanner.svelte
nodes.summary: Svelte component that renders a dismissable notification banner advertising live or open Arena lobby sessions pulled from the arenaStore SSE stream.

nodes.name: ArenaIsland.tsx
nodes.summary: Root React component for the Arena 2D game client — manages Socket.IO connection lifecycle and routes between Lobby, Match, Results, and Spectator scenes based on server-driven state transitions.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses useCallback/useEffect for socket lifecycle management and dispatches to scene sub-components based on a string scene state.

nodes.name: ArenaIsland
nodes.summary: Main Arena React component managing WebSocket connection, lobby/match lifecycle, and scene routing for the game client.

nodes.name: arenaStore.ts
nodes.summary: Svelte writable store and SSE polling logic that tracks the live Arena lobby banner state across the site, using fetch-based streaming instead of EventSource to support Authorization headers.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a custom fetch-based SSE loop (not native EventSource) because EventSource does not support custom request headers for Bearer tokens.

nodes.name: startArenaStream
nodes.summary: Initiates and manages a streaming SSE fetch loop against /api/arena/active, parsing newline-delimited JSON events and updating the banner Svelte store with lobby phase transitions.

nodes.name: ControlsPanel.tsx
nodes.summary: React component providing a tabbed controls/key-bindings panel for the Arena game, allowing players to view and rebind keyboard actions stored in localStorage.

nodes.name: ControlsPanel
nodes.summary: Interactive key-binding panel component with tab navigation, per-action rebinding via keyboard capture, and one-click reset to defaults.

nodes.name: diff.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the Arena applyDiff function, verifying correct state mutation across item/powerup/door/player operations.

nodes.name: diff.ts
nodes.summary: Pure function that applies a compact binary-encoded differential patch (ops array) to an Arena match state object, mutating items, powerups, doors, and player fields in place.

nodes.name: applyDiff
nodes.summary: Applies a server-sent differential patch list to a mutable match state, parsing operation prefixes to add/remove items, powerups, doors, and update player fields.

nodes.name: input.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for Arena input utilities — verifying WASD vector computation, aim angle calculation, and key-binding load/save round-trips.

nodes.name: input.ts
nodes.summary: Arena game input system: manages keyboard/mouse event listeners, computes WASD movement vectors and aim angles, and emits player-input events to the server over Socket.IO on a 50 ms tick loop.

nodes.name: start
nodes.summary: Registers keyboard, mouse, and visibility event listeners and starts a 50 ms input-polling interval that emits player movement/aim state to the server via the provided socket.

nodes.name: mapData.ts
nodes.summary: Static arena map data module exporting map dimensions, solid wall rectangles, and door positions used by the Renderer and diff system.

nodes.name: Renderer.ts
nodes.summary: PixiJS-based 2D renderer for the Arena game, managing player sprites, item/powerup sync, tracer effects, zone overlays, and a ticker loop with variable speed for slow-motion support.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses PixiJS v8 API (app.init async, Graphics method chaining with setFillStyle/setStrokeStyle) and lazy async sprite loading via Assets.load.

nodes.name: Renderer
nodes.summary: PixiJS Application wrapper that initialises the arena canvas, manages player/item/powerup sprites, draws map geometry, and drives the render loop with configurable ticker speed.

nodes.name: sfx.ts
nodes.summary: Procedural Web Audio API sound effects module for the Arena game, generating shot, melee, death, zone-warning, slow-motion, and victory sounds entirely from synthesised oscillators and noise buffers.
nodes.languageNotes: All sounds are synthesised at runtime via the Web Audio API — no audio file assets are required.

nodes.name: Hud.tsx
nodes.summary: Arena in-game HUD React component displaying player health, ammo, active powerups, minimap, kill feed, ping, mute toggle, controls button, and forfeit action.

nodes.name: Hud
nodes.summary: Renders the full in-match HUD overlay with health/ammo bars, minimap positions, kill feed, powerup indicators, ping, mute, controls, and forfeit affordances.

nodes.name: KillFeed.tsx
nodes.summary: Small React component showing a timed kill-feed overlay that automatically expires entries after 4 seconds and limits the visible list to the 6 most recent kills.

nodes.name: LobbyScene.tsx
nodes.summary: Arena lobby scene React component displaying player slots, character selection, countdown timer, and host start-game controls before a match begins.

nodes.name: LobbyScene
nodes.summary: Renders the pre-match lobby with connected player slots, character picker, ready countdown, and conditional host controls.

nodes.name: MatchScene.tsx
nodes.summary: Core in-match scene: initialises the PixiJS Renderer on a canvas, processes server diff messages, drives the HUD overlay, handles input, slow-motion, and exposes forfeit/controls UI.

nodes.name: MatchScene
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the active match: sets up Renderer and input loop, listens to socket diff/event messages, maintains local state for HUD and slow-motion, and renders game canvas plus HUD overlay.

nodes.name: ResultsScene.tsx
nodes.summary: Post-match results scene React component showing final player rankings with kills/deaths, a victory jingle on mount, and rematch/back navigation.

nodes.name: SpectatorScene.tsx
nodes.summary: Arena spectator view that renders a read-only PixiJS game canvas, allows switching between alive players via follow-target, and mirrors the HUD overlay without input controls.

nodes.name: SpectatorScene
nodes.summary: Spectator mode scene that initialises a renderer, applies incoming diffs to local state, supports player-follow selection, and renders an informational HUD without player input.

nodes.name: lobbyTypes.ts
nodes.summary: Canonical Arena protocol type definitions shared between the website client and the arena-server — includes MatchState, PlayerState, all client/server message discriminated unions, and a protocol version constant (CI drift guard: must stay byte-identical with arena-server/src/proto/messages.ts).
nodes.languageNotes: This file is subject to a CI byte-identity check against arena-server/src/proto/messages.ts — any change here must be mirrored there identically.

nodes.name: AssistantBubble.svelte

nodes.name: inbox-actions.test.ts

Receives all state and callbacks from InboxApp via props.

tour.title: System Architecture Overview
tour.description: Begin with the architecture documentation to understand the full scope of this platform: a self-hosted Kubernetes collaboration suite integrating Keycloak SSO, Nextcloud, Collabora, Vaultwarden, LiveKit streaming, a custom Astro+Svelte website with native messaging, and two multiplayer game services. This document maps the Kubernetes component diagram and the network topology before you see a single line of application code.
tour.languageLesson: Kubernetes organises containers into Pods grouped into Namespaces. Traefik acts as the Ingress controller that routes external HTTP/HTTPS traffic to the correct Service by hostname. All platform services live in the 'workspace' namespace; the website gets its own 'website' namespace for isolation.

tour.title: Kubernetes Base Manifest
tour.description: The root Kustomize file is the deployment manifest of the entire platform — it enumerates all 40+ resource YAML files that constitute the workspace namespace. Understanding this file tells you every service that exists and how they are assembled into a coherent deployment unit. From here, 'task workspace:deploy' materialises the full stack with a single command.
tour.languageLesson: Kustomize 'bases' and 'resources' let you compose many YAML manifests into a single deployable bundle. 'configMapGenerator' dynamically creates ConfigMaps from files at deploy time, so realm templates and domain configs never need to be manually synchronised with their sources.

tour.title: Keycloak: The SSO Backbone
tour.description: Keycloak is the identity spine of the entire platform. This Kubernetes manifest deploys Keycloak 26 with a PostgreSQL backend and realm-import entrypoint. Every other service — Nextcloud, Vaultwarden, the website, DocuSeal, Brett, and the arena-server — authenticates through it via OIDC. Understanding Keycloak's placement is essential before reading any authentication code.
tour.languageLesson: OIDC (OpenID Connect) extends OAuth2 with identity tokens. When a user logs in, Keycloak issues a JWT that downstream services verify. The realm JSON imported at boot contains client configurations, role mappings, and realm-level settings — deploying Keycloak without that JSON produces a blank instance with no clients.

tour.title: Authentication in Code
tour.description: auth.ts is the single most-imported file in the codebase — 292 other modules reference it. It implements the complete Keycloak OIDC OAuth2 flow: redirecting to Keycloak for login, exchanging the authorization code for tokens, storing the session in PostgreSQL, and exposing getSession() which every protected API route and Astro page calls. This is the security boundary for the entire Astro website.
tour.languageLesson: OIDC sessions are stored server-side in PostgreSQL (not cookies alone) for security and revocability. The Keycloak admin SDK wrapper in keycloak.ts separates identity-provider administration (user CRUD, role assignment) from the OAuth2 flow — two distinct concerns that are easy to conflate.

tour.title: The Website Entry Point and Layouts
tour.description: The Astro homepage branches on the BRAND_ID environment variable to render either the mentolder or korczewski design, making a single codebase serve two distinct products. AdminLayout.astro and PortalLayout.astro are the two authenticated shells of the platform — the admin panel for coaches and the client portal for users — each enforcing an OIDC session guard before rendering their sidebar navigation.
tour.languageLesson: Astro's server-side rendering executes layout code at request time, so session guards (calling auth.ts getSession()) run before any HTML is sent to the browser. A redirect response from a layout short-circuits the page render entirely — no client-side JavaScript needed for auth protection.

tour.title: The Central Data Hub
tour.description: website-db.ts is the star-topology hub of the platform: 4,176 lines providing 140+ exported functions that cover customers, meetings, projects, time-tracking, coaching sessions, questionnaires, billing, messaging, and more. With a fan-in of 211, it is the second-most-imported file. Rather than scattered ORM models, the platform uses a single, discipline-specific data access layer that keeps SQL close to its domain logic.
tour.languageLesson: This pattern is sometimes called an 'anemic repository' or 'fat DAO' — all queries for a domain in one file rather than per-entity classes. The advantage is discoverability and controlled surface area; the cost is that the file becomes a coordination point. The 140+ functions are organised by domain subdomain within the file.

tour.title: Domain-Specific Database Modules
tour.description: Several domains warrant their own database module extracted from the hub. messaging-db.ts (fan-in: 27) covers the native in-platform chat system. knowledge-db.ts (fan-in: 15) manages pgvector-backed document collections for RAG retrieval. coaching-db.ts and questionnaire-db.ts (fan-in: 20 each) handle the coaching content pipeline and multi-step questionnaire engine respectively. Together they show how the monolithic DB layer decomposes for heavier subsystems.
tour.languageLesson: pgvector extends PostgreSQL with a vector column type and ANN (approximate nearest-neighbour) operators like <=>. Storing embeddings in the same Postgres instance as relational data eliminates a separate vector-database service and lets you JOIN structured metadata with semantic similarity in one query.

tour.title: The AI Assistant Subsystem
tour.description: The platform embeds a Claude-powered sidekick assistant accessible from both the admin panel and the client portal. types.ts defines the type contract for messages, conversation state, and trigger descriptors. actions.ts implements the action registry — a plugin-style system where each assistant action registers itself and declares which user profiles may invoke it. llm.ts orchestrates the full response pipeline: keyword search, RAG retrieval from coaching collections, Claude API call, and streaming.
tour.languageLesson: The action registry pattern (registerAction/executeAction) is a form of inversion of control: the central LLM handler does not know which actions exist — they self-register on module load. This lets new assistant capabilities be added by creating a file and importing its barrel index, without modifying the core chat handler.

tour.title: German Compliance Billing Engine
tour.description: native-billing.ts (fan-in: 15, fan-out: 12) is the GoBD-compliant invoicing engine. It handles customer CRUD, invoice lifecycle (draft → finalised → sent → paid), dunning notices, and DATEV accounting export. invoice-pdf.ts generates branded PDFs with embedded FacturX/ZUGFeRD XML, while einvoice/cii.ts produces UN/CEFACT Cross-Industry Invoice XML for XRechnung — a mandatory e-invoice format for German public-sector clients.
tour.languageLesson: ZUGFeRD and XRechnung are German e-invoice standards based on EN 16931. ZUGFeRD embeds CII XML inside a PDF as an attachment (making the PDF machine-readable); XRechnung is a pure-XML profile used for B2G invoicing. GoBD (German tax code) requires invoices to be tamper-evident and retained for 10 years — once finalised, invoice records must never be overwritten.

tour.title: Arena Multiplayer Server
tour.description: arena-server/src/index.ts bootstraps a standalone Node.js Express + WebSocket server for the browser-based multiplayer arena game. It runs database migrations, wires the WebSocket broadcaster, and starts the HTTP server independently of the Astro website. The game loop lives in tick.ts — a fixed-interval simulation that processes physics, weapons, powerups, and zone captures — driven by the lifecycle.ts lobby state machine.
tour.languageLesson: A fixed-rate game loop (typically 20–60 ticks/second) decouples physics simulation from client frame rates. Each tick, the server advances game state, detects collisions, and broadcasts the delta to all connected clients. Clients render interpolated positions between server ticks — this is why 'tick rate' matters for game feel even in browser games.

tour.title: The Shared Game Protocol
tour.description: arena-server/src/proto/messages.ts defines every WebSocket message type exchanged between the arena server and the website's React game client. The CI pipeline enforces that this file is byte-identical to website/src/components/arena/shared/lobbyTypes.ts — any drift between the two fails the build. This single-source-of-truth contract prevents the silent type mismatches that plague split-process WebSocket APIs.
tour.languageLesson: Keeping protocol types as a duplicated-but-enforced file (rather than a shared npm package) is a common monorepo trade-off: no package publishing, no version skew, and the diff check in CI is trivially easy to write. The alternative — a shared types package — adds publish/install ceremony and can still drift if a developer forgets to bump the version.

tour.title: Brett 3D Game Server
tour.description: brett/server.js is a separate Node.js Express + WebSocket server for the 3D multiplayer systemic-constellation board (Systembrett). With a fan-out of 14, it imports session management, PostgreSQL room persistence, OIDC authentication against Keycloak, and the three.js physics model. Its Dockerfile packages it as a minimal single-stage image deployed as a standalone Kubernetes service.
tour.languageLesson: Running Brett as a separate process (not embedded in the Astro server) means it can be scaled, restarted, and deployed independently. The Astro website proxies WebSocket connections through Traefik — clients never connect directly to brett's port, so the service topology remains hidden behind the ingress layer.

tour.title: LiveKit Streaming Stack
tour.description: The LiveKit manifest deploys the full WebRTC stack: Redis state store, LiveKit server (with hostNetwork and node-affinity for direct UDP access), RTMP Ingress for OBS, and Egress for recording to a PVC. This hostNetwork design is the reason LiveKit requires DNS-pinning and explicit firewall rules — the server binds directly to the node's network interfaces, bypassing Kubernetes' usual virtual networking.
tour.languageLesson: hostNetwork:true removes the virtual-ethernet layer (veth pair + CNI overlay) and gives the pod direct access to the node's network stack. This is required for WebRTC because STUN/TURN probes must reach the server's true IP — NAT traversal breaks if the pod IP is unreachable from outside. The trade-off is that only one hostNetwork pod can bind a given port per node.

tour.title: Data Model and Workflow Reference
tour.description: The datamodel-workflow.md document is an exhaustive cross-domain reference mapping every database table, API route, and business-process step across the entire platform — 1,800 lines covering customers, meetings, billing, coaching, tickets, questionnaires, messaging, and assets. Reading this alongside website-db.ts (Step 6) gives a complete picture of how data flows from user interactions through the API layer into persistent storage.
